tossups.question: One work by this composer opens homophonically with the motif [read slowly] G, G, F, G, A-flat, G, F, G. The fourth section of a keyboard work by this composer is split up into sections such as “the marche of horsemen” and “the flute & the drõme.” It’s not Benjamin Britten, but the musicologist Philip Brett is noted for his study of this composer, and wrote an article comparing a work by this composer to John (*) Taverner’s Missa the Mean. This composer’s three completed masses are for three, four and five voices. This composer is the younger of the two who were granted a 21 year monopoly for the printing of music in England by Queen Elizabeth I. For 10 points, name this Renaissance composer of My Ladye Nevells Booke (“my Lady Nevill’s Book”) and student of Thomas Tallis.
tossups.question_sanitized: One work by this composer opens homophonically with the motif [read slowly] G, G, F, G, A-flat, G, F, G. The fourth section of a keyboard work by this composer is split up into sections such as "the marche of horsemen" and "the flute & the drome." It's not Benjamin Britten, but the musicologist Philip Brett is noted for his study of this composer, and wrote an article comparing a work by this composer to John (*) Taverner's Missa the Mean. This composer's three completed masses are for three, four and five voices. This composer is the younger of the two who were granted a 21 year monopoly for the printing of music in England by Queen Elizabeth I. For 10 points, name this Renaissance composer of My Ladye Nevells Booke ("my Lady Nevill's Book") and student of Thomas Tallis.

packet.name: N – Auburn A, Boston University A, Florida B, Illinois A, Northwestern A, Oxford A, Texas A

tossups.question: A lyricist with this first name lived near the author of First Book of Consort Lessons, Thomas Morley, and worked with the non-blues musician Robert Johnson. The Deller Consort’s album titled for that man with this first name includes “O Mistress Mine” and “Bonny Sweet Robin.” A composer with this first name wrote My Ladye Nevells Booke for the virginal after he and his teacher earned a 21-year monopoly on printed music in his country. A writer with this first name penned the lyrics to songs including “Where the Bee Sucks” and “Sigh No More.” Thomas Tallis taught a composer with this first name and the last name Byrd. For 10 points, give this first name of the lyricist of “Full Fathom Five,” who adapted the “Willow Song” for Desdemona in his play Othello.
tossups.answer_sanitized: William [accept William Shakespeare; accept William Byrd]

For 10 points, give this first name of the lyricist of "Full Fathom Five," who adapted the "Willow Song" for Desdemona in his play Othello.

tossups.question: A text by this writer describes the “left hand path” where a dreadful “old woman sits on a toad-stool” and has “rattlesnakes” for hair instead of tresses. This author inserted himself into a work where he meets an Indian hunter who is so good at hunting bear meat that he’s able to test his theory as to whether a diet of bear meat makes men more virile. An Indian named Ned Bearskin is described in a text in which this author is surrounded by men of “hoggish temper” that he names Puzzlecause, Firebrand, and Shoebrush. This author styled himself “Steddy” in a text that provides more “scandalous” material than a similarly named 1728 non-fiction account, which describes William Dandridge and Richard Fitz-William, two fellow surveyors. For 10 points, name this founder of Richmond, who wrote a “secret” version of his History of the Dividing Line.

For 10 points, name this founder of Richmond, who wrote a "secret" version of his History of the Dividing Line.

tossups.question: Gordon Jacob composed a suite named after this composer that “freely transcribes” six of his pieces for orchestra, though its more famous version is for symphonic wind band. This composer’s Mass for Four Voices was one of three settings he composed for the Mass Ordinary. This composer’s setting of “Ave verum corpus” is written in the G Aeolian mode and interestingly stresses the word “verum” instead of “corpus.” One of this composer’s collections contains a nine-part piece that begins with the section “the soldier summons” and concludes with “the retreat.” That piece, “the battell,” is sandwiched between “the march before the battell” and “the (*) galliarde for the victorie.” This man composed a book of keyboard music that contains ten pavans and ten galliards. Like his teacher, this composer contributed 17 motets to a collection of pieces dedicated to Elizabeth I. For 10 points, name this English composer of My Ladye Nevells Booke who studied under Thomas Tallis.
tossups.question_sanitized: Gordon Jacob composed a suite named after this composer that "freely transcribes" six of his pieces for orchestra, though its more famous version is for symphonic wind band. This composer's Mass for Four Voices was one of three settings he composed for the Mass Ordinary. This composer's setting of "Ave verum corpus" is written in the G Aeolian mode and interestingly stresses the word "verum" instead of "corpus." One of this composer's collections contains a nine-part piece that begins with the section "the soldier summons" and concludes with "the retreat." That piece, "the battell," is sandwiched between "the march before the battell" and "the (*) galliarde for the victorie." This man composed a book of keyboard music that contains ten pavans and ten galliards. Like his teacher, this composer contributed 17 motets to a collection of pieces dedicated to Elizabeth I. For 10 points, name this English composer of My Ladye Nevells Booke who studied under Thomas Tallis.

tossups.question: This composer preceded John Bull and Jan Sweelinck in writing what is thought to be the earliest hexachord fantasia. He employed a “false relation” between an F-sharp in the Superius and an F in the Bassus in the opening phrase of his setting of Ave Verum Corpus, which is in G minor and 4/2 time. He included variations on “Sellinger’s Round” and “Carman’s Whistle” in one instrumental collection. The death of Robert Cecil inspired a pair of dances by him titled for the “Earl of (*) Salisbury”. He and his teacher were granted a 21-year monopoly on printing polyphonic music by Elizabeth I. Ten paired pavanes and galliards appear in a work by him that is, besides the Fitzwilliam Virginal Book, the most important collection of keyboard music of the English Renaissance. For 10 points, name this English Catholic composer, who wrote My Ladye Nevells Booke and co-wrote the Cantiones Sacrae with Thomas Tallis.
tossups.question_sanitized: This composer preceded John Bull and Jan Sweelinck in writing what is thought to be the earliest hexachord fantasia. He employed a "false relation" between an F-sharp in the Superius and an F in the Bassus in the opening phrase of his setting of Ave Verum Corpus, which is in G minor and 4/2 time. He included variations on "Sellinger's Round" and "Carman's Whistle" in one instrumental collection. The death of Robert Cecil inspired a pair of dances by him titled for the "Earl of (*) Salisbury". He and his teacher were granted a 21-year monopoly on printing polyphonic music by Elizabeth I. Ten paired pavanes and galliards appear in a work by him that is, besides the Fitzwilliam Virginal Book, the most important collection of keyboard music of the English Renaissance. For 10 points, name this English Catholic composer, who wrote My Ladye Nevells Booke and co-wrote the Cantiones Sacrae with Thomas Tallis.

tossups.question: This composer provided the complete mass Propers for the major feasts of the Roman liturgy in his 109-piece Gradualia. This composer created a set of eight variations on the tune The Carman's Whistle. An elegy on the death of this composer's mentor concludes with the line "[the mentor] is dead, and Music dies", which is entitled Ye Sacred Muses. Some of this composer's keyboard music is contained in the collection My Ladye Nevells Booke. He was perhaps the most notable composer of consort songs. With his mentor, this composer published Cantiones sacrae after they were given a monopoly on the printing and marketing of music by Elizabeth I; that mentor was Thomas Tallis. For 10 points, identify this Renaissance English composer of lots of virginal music.

Some of this composer's keyboard music is contained in the collection My Ladye Nevells Booke. He was perhaps the most notable composer of consort songs. With his mentor, this composer published Cantiones sacrae after they were given a monopoly on the printing and marketing of music by Elizabeth I; that mentor was Thomas Tallis. For 10 points, identify this Renaissance English composer of lots of virginal music.

bonuses.leadin: This composer set the Book of Lamentations for use in Holy Week services during the 1560s. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer of the motet Spem in Alium whose setting of Psalm 2 became the basis of a Ralph (rafe) Vaughan Williams "fantasia."

Tallis's music marked the transition away from this Renaissance technique, in which a single syllable is held across a note spanning multiple pitches. Anthony Heilbut noted that this technique has re-entered popular music due to heavy use in gospel songs and a preference for it on American Idol.

This student of Tallis, who shared a 21-year patent on publishing polyphonic music in England, wrote Ye Sacred Muses to commemorate Tallis's death. This author composed the collection My Ladye Nevells Booke (Lady Nevell's book) for virginal.

parts_sanitized: Name this composer of the motet Spem in Alium whose setting of Psalm 2 became the basis of a Ralph (rafe) Vaughan Williams "fantasia."

bonuses.leadin: Despite being Catholic and publishing Mass Propers in his Gradualia, this composer managed to prosper under a Protestant regime for decades. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Catholic English composer, who co-composed a set of Cantiones Sacrae along with an older composer who may have been his teacher. Along with that older composer, he held a monopoly on printing music for 21 years.

Like much Renaissance music, Byrd’s motet Aspice Domine de sede (“AH-spee-che do-mee-ne”) is a polyphonic piece based around one of these pre-existing melodies, known by a Latin term. These melodies were usually placed in the tenor voice.

This English queen granted Byrd and Tallis the monopoly on music printing, and was the dedicatee of their Cantiones Sacrae. Although she was a Protestant, she kept Byrd around in order to raise her court’s reputation.
answers: William Byrd (The older composer is Thomas Tallis.)
answers_sanitized: William Byrd (The older composer is Thomas Tallis.)

This English queen granted Byrd and Tallis the monopoly on music printing, and was the dedicatee of their Cantiones Sacrae. Although she was a Protestant, she kept Byrd around in order to raise her court's reputation.

bonuses.leadin: This conductor began their professional career convening a choir to perform Monteverdi’s Vespers of 1610 in the chapel of King’s College, Cambridge. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this conductor of the Monteverdi Choir and the English Baroque Soloists, an advocate of historically informed performance and the use of period instruments.

The members of this family of stringed instruments were the main ones used in Renaissance and Baroque music. Instruments in this family, in contrast to the family most common today, have sloping shoulders, five or more strings, and are tuned in fourths rather than fifths.

Among the period recordings of Gardiner’s Monteverdi Choir is the the album Vigilate! Consisting of music from English Catholic composers, its title piece is by this student of Thomas Tallis, who also wrote My Ladye Nevells Booke.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name these composers of battaglia (bah-TAHL-yah), a genre of 16th-century music depicting combat.
parts: This composer made some of the first uses of pizzicato and tremolo to depict the fight between a Christian knight and a Saracen warrior woman in The Combat Between Tancred and Clorinda. He also composed nine books of madrigals and the early opera L’Orfeo.

This English composer included the nine-movement piece “The Battle” and a mock battaglia depicting a couples’ game called “The Barley Break” in a set of 42 keyboard pieces dedicated to “My Ladye Nevell.”

This French composer’s “La Guerre,” depicting the Franco-Venetian rout at Marignano (mah-rin-YAH-no), and “Le Chant des oiseaux” (leh SHAHNT deh wah-ZOHZ), depicting birdsong, are among his 286 chansons, many of them programmatic.
answers_sanitized: Claudio Monteverdi [or Claudio Giovanni Antonio Monteverdi]

bonuses.leadin: This composer wrote the F minor Aria Sebaldina variations for harpsichord as part of his Hexachordum Apollinis. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer and organist whose other works include the suites for chamber ensembles called Musical Delight and the keyboard Musical Thoughts of Dying.
Pachelbel’s most popular composition is a D major three-voice piece in this genre, which he scored for three violins and basso continuo and paired with a gigue.
This earlier organist of the Renaissance era studied under Thomas Tallis at the Chapel Royal in London. He wrote the keyboard pieces in the collection My Ladye Nevells Booke.

bonuses.leadin: There is reason to believe that this man was a student of Thomas Tallis, with whom he published the Cantiones sacrae. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Elizabethan English composer who included keyboard music in his My Ladye Nevells Booke.
Several of Byrd's pieces referred to the notes of a hexachord as "Ut, re, mi, fa, sol, la," in a precursor to this note-labeling system in which "Ut" was replaced by "Do."
Byrd wrote dozens of these often-sacred vocal compositions that emerged in the late Middle Ages. Byrd's colleague Thomas Tallis wrote one of these called Spem in alium for forty distinct voices.

bonuses.leadin: This composer of the motet collection Cantiones sacrae was mentored by the composer of the forty-part Spem in alium. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Virginalist composer who collected 42 keyboard works in My Ladye Nevell’s Booke.
William Byrd was mentored by Thomas Tallis, who wrote a melody that served as the basis for a fantasia by this more recent composer. This man also wrote The Lark Ascending.

bonuses.leadin: This composer rose to prominence despite his devout Catholicism, which was reflected in his liturgical Gradualia. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer of the Cantiones Sacrae, whose work with the viol consort also marked the development of the fantasia.

Byrd appears alongside John Bull, Peter Philips, and Giles Farnaby in the Fitzwilliam Book, a collection of pieces for this late Renaissance instrument in which wooden shafts plucked strings that ran parallel to the keyboard within a usually legless case.

The virginal was succeeded as the most popular keyboard instrument by this one, which was the instrument for which about 542 of Domenico Scarlatti's sonatas and all of Bach’s Brandenburg concertos were composed.

parts: Name this organist at the Chapel Royal who composed the English collection Psalms, Songs, and Sonnets and the Latin motets of Gradualia, also known for studying under the composer of Spem in Alium.

Byrd 's teacher Thomas Tallis composed a theme that became subject to a notable fantasia by this later English composer. He also wrote a fantasia on “Greensleaves,” the Sinfonia Antarctica, and the viola concerto Flos Campi.

bonuses.leadin: The theme for this piece is in the Phrygian mode and was originally part of a hymnal for Archbishop Matthew Parker. For 10 points each:
parts: A certain composer’s setting of “Why Fum’th in Fight” provides the basis of what piece that splits a string orchestra into three parts of differing size?
Fantasia on a Theme by Thomas Tallis is by this English composer of Fantasia on Greensleves and The Lark Ascending.
This contemporary of Thomas Tallis collaborated with him on a series of motets called Cantiones Sacrae. His keyboard output includes My Ladye Nevells Booke.
answers: Fantasia on a Theme by Thomas Tallis [or Tallis Fantasia]
